title: Calamarian Revolt

{{Quote|We forced the Empire off our world once. They fought with blasters and fighters, destroying cities at will. We fought with whatever we had - kitchen utensils, industrial tools, our bare hands. We won that time.|[[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]|<ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
The '''Calamarian Revolt''', also known as the '''Great Revolt''', was the uprising in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] which saw the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Species/Legends|species]] rise up against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who had been occupying their [[Dac/Legends|homeworld]] for nearly two decades and drove the Empire offworld. The resulting battle liberated the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] from the Empire and led to their eventually joining in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The liberation of Dac was notably one of the few successes of the Rebellion's otherwise-disastrous first campaign, [[Operation Domino]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|Together Juno, Bail, and I brought Dac into the Alliance fold—that's a story you may have heard before. But you may not have grasped how important a role Juno played. She fought alongside me at Heurkea and engineered the plan that sent the 181st TIE Fighter Squadron off on a bantha hunt, publicly embarrassed Tarkin, and gave our people and the Quarren the courage to rise up against the Empire. The campaign at Dac would become a template for our efforts—a small, strategic attack with enormous symbolic value.|Gial Ackbar, in [[Ackbar: Wartime Correspondence, volume 8]]|<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
During the nearly two decades of Imperial control of the planet,<ref name="GatORW" /> the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] maintained a low scale resistance. One of the founders of the resistance, the Mon Calamarian [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], had spent the preceding years working with other like minded people to oppose the Imperial control of their homeworld.<ref name="HttES">''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> The techniques of the early resistance were not openly violent or apparent- ranging from industrial slowdowns, acts of sabotage, and defiant protests. The Empire struck back- though with propaganda rather than force. Imperial xenobiologist [[Obo Rin]] published several works claiming that the Empire had first discovered Mon Calamari, in an effort to erase the worlds past history and connection to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="GatORW" /> Still, the Mon Calamari and Quarren refused to submit to Imperial rule. Even the long animosity between the Quarren and the Mon Calamari was set aside, as Quarren leaders rallied their people beside their "planet-brothers." Ironically, one of these Quarren leaders was [[Seggor Tels]], who had been led by jealousy of the Mon Calamari to sabotage Calamari's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]] to pave the way for the [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|Imperial conquest of the planet]].<ref name="TSoST" />

Early during [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Ackbar was freed from Imperial enslavement during a Rebel [[Liberation of Gial Ackbar|attack]] on [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] shuttle.<ref name="HttES" /> Then, in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] team, consisting of [[Senator/Legends|former Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]], and the [[Holodroid/Legends|holodroid]] [[PROXY]] infiltrated the [[Heurkea Floating City]]. The [[Heurkea operation|mission]] was supported by Gial Ackbar and Seggor Tels's Mon Calamari and Quarren freedom fighters. The operation resulted in the capture of Imperial [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Evir Derricote]] and in the public humiliation of Imperial forces, especially Grand Moff Tarkin and the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]].<ref name="TFU2n">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' novelization]]</ref> The success of this operation gave the Mon Calamari and Quarren enough courage to revolt against their oppressors.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

Over the time, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was tired of the acts of defiance.<ref name="TSWS" /> Following the Heurkea operation,<ref name="SoR" /> the Empire decided to make an example by executing an orbital bombardment of three floating cities.<ref name="TSWS" /> The order of Emperor Palpatine was executed by [[Admiral]] [[Garrus Tharydine]], the Imperial commander overseeing the occupation of Mon Calamari.<ref name="TFUCG" /> The attacks were swift and severe, incinerating the cities and their inhabitants. It was believed that this show of force would cow the world's population. In reality, it provided the catalyst for open rebellion to Imperial rule.<ref name="TSWS" />

==The Revolt==
{{Quote|The Mon Calamari have thrown off Imperial control and attacked the Empire's fleets orbiting above them.|[[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]|<ref name="EaW">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref>}}
Imperial occupation forces were caught off guard by the reaction of the Mon Calamari and Quarren. Across the planet Dac the long oppressed fought back. At first, many fought Imperial forces with nothing more than kitchen utensils or hand tools fashioned into weapons.<ref name="TSWS" /> Yet, thanks to early efforts of the resistance, numerous secret weapons caches that had been secreted away over the previous two decades were deployed against Imperial forces. Workers in the [[Mon Calamari Orbital Shipyards]] sabotaged Imperial warships in dock and diverted captured materials to the resistance. Stolen materials were used in secret ship-construction sites located beneath the seas of Calamari and outfitted privateer craft which plagued Imperial starships. Caught completely by surprise by the ferocity of the rebellion, Imperial forces abandoned the planet entirely.<ref name="GatORW" />

During the retreat, Imperial forces loaded their ships with Mon Calamari [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] before leaving the planet. However, these vessels were [[Rescue at Mon Calamari|intercepted]] at the edge of the [[Mon Calamari system/Legends|Calamari system]] by a Rebel Alliance starfleet comprised of several [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] - the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], the [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], and the [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] - who had escaped the Imperial conquest of the planet decades earlier and joined the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] in order to participate in the liberation of their home planet.<ref name="SoR" /> In the ensuing battle, many Imperial ships were destroyed and most of the slaves freed. Though the Empire suffered many losses, [[Imperial pilot]] [[DS-61-4]] managed to survive the battle.<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=A New Hope Limited|cardname=DS-61-4}}</ref> By the end of the Calamarian Revolt, Imperial forces had all but abandoned the entire [[Calamari sector]].<ref name="TEA-">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref><ref name="SoR" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|When we resisted, our cities were attacked, to set an example for the rest of the galaxy... But this experience only hardened my people's will to resist... And transformed them into formidable fighters for the Alliance!|Admiral Ackbar|<ref name="Dark Empire 2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref>}}
The united peoples of Mon Calamari had finally achieved their long sought goal of freedom from enslavement by the Empire. Their victory became a beacon of hope for the downtrodden peoples of the galaxy.<ref name="GatORW" /> However, the planet Mon Calamari remained officially neutral<ref name="TEA-" /> as the [[Mon Calamari Council]] did not vote to fully join the Alliance to Restore the Republic, in part due to Quarren fears of an alliance with another Human dominated group;<ref name="GatORW" /> however, Ackbar was still able to convince his people to join the Rebel Alliance en masse.<ref name="HttES" /> Mon Calamari starships joined the Alliance and the Mon Calamari Shipyards turned their production to build warships at high speed.<ref name="GatORW" /> Mon Calamari immediately became the premier source of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] for the Rebellion.<ref name="SoR" />

After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Ackbar and several members of the Mon Calamari Council were [[Battle of Daluuj|rescued]] on [[Daluuj/Legends|Daluuj]]<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> by the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]].<ref name="It's a Trap">"[[It's a Trap]]"</ref> Thus, in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the Mon Calamari Council finally decided to make the planet become a full member of the Alliance.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

The Calamarian Revolt was one of the few successes of [[Operation Domino]], the Rebel Alliance's earliest campaign against the Empire. On nearly every other world where the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military]] stoked revolt, the uprisings were swiftly crushed by the Galactic Empire.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" /> In the aftermath of the liberation of Mon Calamari, the Mon Calamari and Quarren swiftly fortified their [[Calamari sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="SoR" /> The [[Calamari Defense Force]] was established<ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook" /> and Rebel warships joined the defense of the Calamari sector.<ref name="GatORW" />

An Imperial fleet later tried to reconquer the system, but it was [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Galactic Civil War)|defeated]] by Admiral Ackbar.<ref name="EaW" /> Because of the limited number of Imperial warships in the region,<ref name="GatORW" /> [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Therbon/Legends|Therbon]], the ruler of the [[Maldrood Oversector/Legends|Maldrood Oversector]] that included Mon Calamari Space was unable to retake Mon Calamari in the following years.<ref name="SoR" /> After the liberation of Mon Calamari, the [[Destabilization]] branch of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Calamari Project|infiltrated]] the Quarren city of [[Morjanssik]]. Imperial agents aimed to resume the civil war between the Mon Calamari and Quarren but they were stopped by Rebel operatives.<ref name="DitU" /> Over the years, Grand Moff Therbon grew frustrated with his inability to retake Dac and finally ordered the [[Bombardment of Ruisto|bombardment]] of the Mon Calamari colony world of [[Ruisto]] in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].<ref name="SoR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The release of the game ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' has caused some continuity problems in regards to the liberation of Mon Calamari. A level on the Imperial campaign of the game shows a much [[Ambush at Mon Calamari|different version of the battle]], set right before the Battle of Yavin. The battle itself contradicts numerous previously established sources, including the ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'', ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''. The mission is most likely only designed for game play, not to represent the actual historical liberation of Calamari.
